question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause all historical block indices to be globally rewritten by injecting a crafted RecordFile with a manipulated `getIndex()` value that triggers `repository.updateIndex(offset)` with an attacker-controlled offset, causing every block number in `record_file` to shift and breaking block-number-based transaction lookups for all users?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with `version >= VERSION` (v6) while the last stored file has `version < VERSION` (v5), setting `getIndex()` to an extreme value so that `offset = recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1` becomes a large positive or negative number, causing `updateIndex(offset)` to apply `UPDATE record_file SET index = index + offset` to every row and completely scrambling the canonical block ordering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a WHERE clause in `UPDATE record_file SET index = index + ?1` (called from `updateIndex`) to shift ALL historical block numbers simultaneously, making previously-valid block references in external systems (explorers, dApps) point to wrong transactions?

[File: 'File Name: 'importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the v5-to-v6 version transition check multiple times by repeatedly submitting files that alternate between version < VERSION and version >= VERSION, causing `updateIndex` to be called repeatedly with different offsets and progressively corrupting all stored block indices?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user set `recordFile.getIndex()` equal to `lastRecordFile.getIndex() + 1` so that `offset = 0` is bypassed (offset != 0 check), then on a subsequent file set a large index to trigger a massive offset, effectively deferring and amplifying the block number rewrite?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile whose items have out-of-order `consensusTimestamp` values, causing `recordItemListener.onItem()` to persist transactions in a non-chronological sequence and reorganizing the apparent transaction history stored in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with duplicate `consensusTimestamp` values across multiple `RecordItem` objects, causing the same logical time slot to be associated with multiple different transactions and corrupting the uniqueness invariant of the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where `getConsensusEnd()` is set to a future timestamp, causing `applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(new RecordFileParsedEvent(this, recordFile.getConsensusEnd()))` to fire with a future consensus end before any items are processed, misleading downstream listeners (e.g., `HistoricalBalanceService`) into believing the chain has advanced further than it has?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `RecordFileParsedEvent` is published at the start of `doParse` (line 138) before any `recordItemListener.onItem()` calls complete, causing event consumers to read an inconsistent database state where the record file's consensus end is registered but its transactions are not yet persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile with a `consensusEnd` that is earlier than the `consensusEnd` of the last stored file, bypassing the `shouldParse` check (which only compares `consensusEnd >= consensusStart`) and inserting a file that appears to predate existing history, effectively prepending fabricated transactions to the record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Retryable` annotation on `parse(RecordFile)` to cause a partially-processed RecordFile to be retried after a transient failure, resulting in `recordItemListener.onItem()` being called twice for the same `RecordItem` and inserting duplicate transaction records that distort the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated retries via `@Retryable` by crafting a RecordFile that causes a recoverable exception on every attempt except the last, and during each retry the `parserContext.clear()` in the `finally` block discards partial state, causing some transactions to be permanently omitted from the history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `synchronized` keyword on `parse(RecordFile)` combined with `@Retryable` to cause a denial-of-parse scenario where a malicious file holds the lock during all retry backoff periods, blocking legitimate files from being processed and creating a gap in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `List<RecordFile>` where files are ordered with decreasing `consensusStart` values, exploiting the `shouldParse` check (`previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart()`) to silently skip legitimate files in the batch, creating gaps in the persisted transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch `List<RecordFile>` where the first file has a `consensusEnd` equal to or greater than the `consensusStart` of the second file, causing the second file to be silently skipped via `streamFile.clear()`, permanently omitting those transactions from the mirror node's history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch `parse(List<RecordFile>)` path where `doFlush` is only called on the last successfully parsed file, causing all intermediate `RecordFile` objects' data to be committed under the last file's metadata, misattributing transactions to the wrong block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile whose `getItems()` stream returns items in a different order than their `consensusTimestamp` ordering, causing `logIndex` (AtomicInteger incremented per item) to be assigned out of consensus-timestamp order, corrupting the log index sequence used for EVM log ordering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where `getItems()` contains items that pass `dateRangeFilter.filter()` interspersed with items that do not, causing `logIndex` to be incremented only for filtered-in items, creating non-contiguous log indices that break EVM log ordering assumptions for smart contract event consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile with a `dateRangeFilter` boundary that excludes the first N transactions but includes the rest, causing `recordFile.setCount(count.get())` to reflect only the filtered subset, making the stored `count` field inconsistent with the actual number of transactions in the file and misleading block explorers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept(RecordItem)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile containing a large number of contract transactions with extreme `gasUsed` values, causing `gasUsed += result.getGasUsed()` to overflow a `long`, resulting in a negative or incorrect `gasUsed` stored on the `RecordFile` and corrupting the block's gas accounting metadata?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.update(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with contract results that produce a `logsBloom` collision via `logsBloom.or(DomainUtils.toBytes(result.getBloom()))`, causing the stored bloom filter to falsely indicate the presence of log topics from other transactions, misleading event-filter queries and reorganizing the apparent event history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept(RecordItem)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where `recordItem.isTopLevel()` returns false for all items, causing `RecordItemAggregator.accept()` to skip all items and `aggregator.update(recordFile)` to set `gasUsed = 0` and an empty `logsBloom`, erasing the block's gas and event metadata even though transactions were processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile where `getSidecars()` returns a large collection of sidecar records with manipulated metadata, causing `parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars())` to persist incorrect sidecar data that misrepresents the contract execution history associated with specific transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where `parserContext.add(recordFile)` is called with a RecordFile whose `index` field has been set to a value that conflicts with an existing block number in the database, causing a silent overwrite or constraint violation that corrupts the block-to-transaction mapping?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: shouldParse() in AbstractStreamFileParser] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with `consensusStart` set to a value less than or equal to the `consensusEnd` of the last stored file, causing `shouldParse` to log a warning and call `streamFile.clear()` without throwing an exception, silently discarding the file and creating a permanent gap in the transaction history?
